item.title: Rivian CEO’s $403mn pay package dwarfs those of top US car bosses
item.description: Founder of electric-truck maker earns about 13 times more than next best-paid American auto executive

item.title: Honda and the power of (broken) dreams
item.description: A brilliant and adventurist beacon for Japanese industry in the past is now stumbling

item.title: Paramount and Warner Bros to be nearly 50% foreign-owned after $111bn takeover
item.description: Filings reveal the extent of deal’s reliance on Middle Eastern sovereign-wealth investors

item.title: US Supreme Court appears split on safety labels for Bayer weedkiller Roundup
item.description: Case has emerged as one of the biggest ongoing multibillion-dollar corporate tort claims

item.title: FirstFT: China orders Meta to unwind $2bn acquisition of AI group Manus
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Italy extradites alleged Chinese spy to US, and German chancellor says Washington being ‘humiliated’ by Iran

item.title: Blue Owl investors shun cut-price exit offer from Boaz Weinstein
item.description: Offer from Saba Capital to buy shares of private credit fund at 65 cents on the dollar fails to attract sellers

item.title: German energy giant nears £600mn deal to buy UK supplier Ovo
item.description: A takeover, which would create a combined entity serving about 9.6mn customers, could be reached within weeks

item.title: Wizz Air accuses Gulf airlines of putting politics before safety
item.description: Chief executive József Váradi claims carriers are ‘operating based on political pressure’ in highly unusual criticism

item.title: DR Congo creates $100mn ‘Mining Guard’ to protect critical minerals
item.description: Security force backed by US and UAE aims to drive investment in the African nation’s resource sector

item.title: Meta’s Chinese stumble suggests a declining tolerance for shades of grey
item.description: Tech-related capital flows have benefited from decades of ambiguity, but AI changes the calculus

item.title: Google staff urge chief executive to block US military AI use
item.description: Over 560 employees sign open letter to Sundar Pichai following the Pentagon’s clash with Anthropic

item.title: The EU’s methane regulation could spark an energy crisis
item.description: Its extraterritorial, prescriptive and paperwork-heavy requirements will restrict access to supply options

item.title: OpenAI and Microsoft loosen ties in revised AI deal
item.description: Companies redraw $135bn alliance as ChatGPT maker seeks greater independence to increase revenues

item.title: Hedge fund Jain Global plans to return cash to investors
item.description: Eponymous firm launched by Bobby Jain will instead manage money exclusively for his alma mater Millennium

item.title: Developer says UK insisted on ‘Rolls-Royce’ upgrades for post-Grenfell works
item.description: Urban Splash is fighting government’s bid to recover £48mn in safety costs on buildings in Manchester

item.title: Is Tim Cook’s departure a canary in the coal mine for tech stocks?
item.description: He might have stepped down as Apple CEO at just the right time

item.title: Why flying less won’t do much to ease the oil crisis
item.description: Crude demand weakens when not only jet fuel but other product prices rise too

item.title: Shell buys Canadian shale producer ARC for $16bn
item.description: Deal will boost oil major’s production at a time when investors have queried the company’s growth

item.title: United chief hits out at American Airlines for refusing merger talks
item.description: Scott Kirby criticises rival for ‘closing the door’ on deal he believes would have created jobs

item.title: Adidas gains marathon dominance with 97-gramme ‘supershoes’
item.description: Sabastian Sawe broke 2-hour barrier wearing German brand’s $500 trainers

item.title: Pirates on the prowl again off Somalia’s coast
item.description: Maritime authorities and intelligence agencies say four ships seized in past week

item.title: China’s minerals come with coercion but they do come cheap
item.description: The US has no geoeconomic answer to Beijing controlling its trading partners via manipulating supply chains

item.title: Vinted hits €8bn valuation after EQT-led share sale
item.description: Second-hand marketplace sells €880mn of existing shares to investors including Teachers’ Venture Growth and Schroders Capital

item.title: Should private credit be stressing out insurers?
item.description: Apparently not

item.title: Five printers that are actually cool (and not annoying)
item.description: Imaginative ways to make an impression

item.title: FirstFT: Alleged gunman to appear in court
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: China blocks Meta takeover of Manus and Sam Altman versus Elon Musk trial begins

item.title: Former Apollo risk chief says some new life insurers could struggle in downturn
item.description: Chak Raghunathan cites heavy reliance on untested private credit and newer savings products that are vulnerable to withdrawals

item.title: China blocks Meta’s $2bn purchase of AI group Manus
item.description: Regulators had reviewed whether deal violated Beijing’s investment rules

item.title: China warns EU over proposed ‘Made in Europe’ law
item.description: Beijing threatens unspecified retaliation over fears that bill to strengthen bloc’s industry will hurt its companies

item.title: Italy approves extradition of alleged Chinese hacker to US
item.description: Xu Zewei accused of industrial espionage over suspected effort to steal Covid-19 vaccine secrets
